title: NOC 22303 – Construction Estimators | GTR Immigration
description: Construction Estimators are in strong demand across Canada, driven by residential-housing growth, commercial development, infrastructure renewal, and major public-sector construction programs nationwide. Classified under NOC 22303 (TEER 2), this occupation aligns well with:

classification.teer: 2 – College diploma or apprenticeship normally required

duties: Prepare cost estimates for materials, labour & equipment
Advise on tendering procedures & contract awards
employment_requirements: Three-year college diploma in civil or construction-engineering technology or extensive skilled-trade experience
Certification from the Canadian Institute of Quantity Surveyors (usually required)
job_outlook: This balanced outlook still strongly favours candidates with:
pathways: Advancement into project controls, commercial management, or senior-estimating roles
additional_information: Canadian employers actively recruit construction-cost professionals for:

Residential & condominium developmentsCommercial & industrial buildsHighway & transit projectsElectrical & mechanical contracting firmsGovernment infrastructure departmentsLarge engineering-procurement-construction firms
Annual salaries typically range between $40,500–$94,300 CAD, depending on province, project size, certification level, and specialization.
